[15] Faded purple carpet, neon lighting and arcade cabinets fill this large room. ▶Toward the back, a small stage, light and sound system for concerts. ▶A tiny back storage room has been painted green to double as a green room. �Music equipment is stored here. �Multiple large CO2 tanks are stored near an industrial fog machine. 11. MOOTSY’S ARCADE & MUSIC Bright buzzing fluorescent lights, small chairs, a red take-a-number and a help desk. ▶Every inch of this space is wired with cameras and audio equipment (live feed to the break room computer). ▶A small “research” station is set up in the storage closet. ▶One wall is dominated by a corkboard of local legends and myths. �The word BELOW THE MALL circled, witharrows pointing to many different subterranean based legends -Whisper Card (p. 37). �Pictures of Federal Agents in and around Carpenter. “Is THE BUREAU involved?” is underlined heavily. 9. DMV EXPRESS The mall’s central attraction. ▶A DJ booth is next to the enter/exit panels. ▶Benches are affixed in front of a small service window. �Through the opening, racks and racks of rentable roller skates are visible. 10. SKATE WORLD ROLLER RINK Rows of poorly-lit employee lockers, a couple old couches and scattered folding tables. ▶The flimsy combination locks can be broken with tools or leverage. ▶If searching random lockers, roll (or choose) 3 pieces of Starting Gear (p. 33). Billy's Locker: Flecks of mud on the exterior - (Clue). ▶Small planner lists an appointment from two days ago with Mr. Neil (p. 6) circled in red. ▶Muddy boots and gloves (examination reveals flecks of blood and orange fuzz) - Whisper Card (p. 37). Emmy’s Locker: STAY OUT written in striking letters. The lock is stronger than standard. ▶The door has an unusual resistance and an audible click, if opened. It is rigged with a crossbow trap (d6 damage). ▶“WARNED YOU” written in neon spray paint. Logan’s Locker: A weird rune is carved on this locker. ▶The inside is an evidence board filled with sketches, articles, printouts and a mass of red string. ▶Two modified walkie talkies hang from the coat hooks. Bruce’s Locker: Noticeably shinier and cleaner than the rest. ▶A notebook of observations about the mall. ▶One section details Mr. Neil’s odd behavior. ▶Another notes the areas where mud has been tracked: � Basement entrance. �3 vent openings (near vents that have been acting up). �The loading dock. ▶His notes imply that Mr. Neilis the main culprit - (Clue). 12. BREAK ROOM LEVEL 2 [16] Level 2 // Employee lockers: It’s like shopping in other people’s stuff. BUSINESS ASSOCIATION EMMY’S SWORDS SECURITY BREAK ROOM Level 2 // The cameras in Security are a great way to build tension and give information. [17] MONITORING ROOM ▶Large dark room filled with old, rewired screens. ▶ Hudson (p. 7) is holed up here but avoids looking at the screens. ▶ Doom Clock % (p. 2) chance any given feed has gone dark. ▶ Watching the Feeds: �Time watching reveals two Childrenof Ammon(p. 9) on different screensat the same time. �A Child of Ammon (p. 9) mutilating a NPC before dragging them offscreen - Whisper Card (p. 37). �Later, the NPC is adamant nothing is wrong. Only in “death” will the monster reveal itself. ▶An older microphone and keypad broadcasts over the speakers throughout the mall and shops. Computer terminal keeps backups and can play multiple feeds concurrently on the screens. ▶ If searching for specific information, the only risk is time spent looking for info that could be caught on film. ▶ If searching randomly, roll on table: An early target of most factions. Multiple parties will be vying for resources here by DOOM 5 (p. 2). ▶Divided into three rooms. SMALL FRONT OFFICE ▶Two desks stacked high with paperwork. ▶Security lockers. �4 batons (d6). �A flak vest (+1 Armor). �4 handcuffs. ▶A bright red, mostly-full first aid kit hangs on the wall. ▶Lockbox filled with contraband. ▶ Hudson’s locker: �The inside is wallpapered with a vision board, a smiling Hudson at its center. ▶ Terry’s locker: �2 broken skateboards. �CCTV printout of three teens skating, two of the figures crossed off in red. BATHROOM ▶A retrofitted shower toilet stall. ▶Hidden inside the toilet tank are 4 syringes of a mystery substance. �A homemade drug cocktail. Injecting a full syringe into a REPLACED (p. 8) causes them to showvisible changes. 13. SECURITY d10SECURITY FEEDS 01-04An NPC taking out cameras. 05-06 Mallrats using the vents to get around the mall. 07-09An NPC hiding valuable resources. 10-12Billy living out of the loading dock. 13-14 Mr. Neil leaving a van running in the parking lot - Whisper Card (p. 37). 15-17 Mr. Neil/Billy dragging a still moving body bag into a vent - Whisper Card (p. 37). 18-19 Mr. Neil and Billy bringing equipment to the lower basement - Whisper Card (p. 37). 20 A Child of Ammon (p. 9) attacking an NPC (they show up later “safe”). Small waiting area with an imposing dark wood desk in the middle. ▶A binder filled with complaints sits on the desk. ▶A door leads to a back meeting room with a small kitchenette and 3 whiteboards. �Extensive notes detail each business, owner, multiple employees and customers. �Studying it reveals general info on prominent NPCs, as well Maggie's (p. 6) conjectures on their weaknesses and responses topressure - Whisper Card (p. 37). 14. BUSINESS ASSOCIATION [18] Level 2 // Emmy is bad ass and a great ally. Bonus points if weapons are used with rollerskates. The region’s premier weapons shop. ▶Emmy has reinforced her store (no glass fronting). ▶With proper tools the rear access door may be prised open. �But it is boobytrapped with a barbed crossbow bolt (d8). ▶ Emmy(p. 8) will be holed up, ready to attack anyone who tries to forcefully enter. ▶She is open to trade. She is willing to trade weapons for: �Multiple days worth of food. �High quality tools.. �The extra copies of her shop key from maintenance,
